children.text: Greeting and Purpose
Hi, Yaming. What are you doing?
children.speaker: Emma
children.cn: 嗨，亚明。你在做什么？
children.notes: What are you doing? 用于询问正在进行的动作。
children.statement: 这句话询问的是过去发生的事情。
children.explanation: 这句话使用的是现在进行时，询问的是现在正在做的事情。

children.text: Hi, Emma. I'm starting a test on animals and plants.
children.speaker: Yaming
children.cn: 嗨，艾玛。我正在开始一项关于动植物的测试。
children.notes: starting a test = 开始一场测试
children.statement: 这句话使用了现在进行时。
children.explanation: I'm starting 表示正在进行的动作。

children.text: Sounds fun. Let's take it together.
children.speaker: Emma
children.cn: 听起来很有趣。我们一起做吧。
children.notes: Sounds fun = 听起来很有趣；Let's = Let us
children.statement: Sounds fun 意思是“听起来很无聊”。
children.explanation: fun 的意思是“有趣”。

children.text: Animal Trivia
OK. First question. What's the largest animal in the world?
children.speaker: Yaming
children.cn: 好的。第一个问题。世界上最大的动物是什么？
children.statement: largest 是 large 的最高级形式。
children.explanation: 形容词最高级通常在词尾加 -est。

children.text: It's the blue whale, right?
children.speaker: Emma
children.cn: 是蓝鲸，对吧？
children.statement: right 在句末表示“对吧”。
children.explanation: 在句末加 right 可以表示反问确认。

children.text: Right. It's also the heaviest animal on earth. Next question. Which animal runs the fastest?
children.speaker: Yaming
children.cn: 对。它也是地球上最重的动物。下一个问题。哪种动物跑得最快？
children.notes: heaviest = 最重的；fastest = 最快的
children.statement: heaviest 是 heavy 的最高级。
children.explanation: heavy 以辅音字母+y结尾，变y为i加est。

children.text: Oh wait! I just read about it. The cheetah runs the fastest.
children.speaker: Emma
children.cn: 哦等等！我刚读到过。猎豹跑得最快。
children.statement: cheetah 是一种鸟类。
children.explanation: cheetah 是猎豹，属于哺乳动物。

children.text: Right again! And I think it's the most beautiful animal in the world.
children.speaker: Yaming
children.cn: 又对了！而且我认为它是世界上最美丽的动物。
children.statement: beautiful 的最高级是在词尾加 -est。
children.explanation: 多音节形容词最高级要在前面加 most。

children.text: I completely agree with you.
children.speaker: Emma
children.cn: 我完全同意你的看法。
children.statement: completely 是副词，修饰 agree。
children.explanation: 副词可以修饰动词。

children.text: Plant Trivia
Now let's answer the questions about plants. What is the oldest plant in the world?
children.speaker: Yaming
children.cn: 现在我们来回答关于植物的问题。世界上最古老的植物是什么？
children.statement: oldest 是 old 的最高级。
children.explanation: old 的最高级是 oldest。

children.text: Hmm, I'm not sure. Do you have any idea?
children.speaker: Emma
children.cn: 嗯，我不确定。你知道吗？
children.notes: not sure = 不确定；have any idea = 有主意/知道吗
children.statement: I'm not sure 意思是“我确定”。
children.explanation: not sure 表示不确定。

children.text: Well, I think moss is one of the oldest plants. It appeared on earth around 470 million years ago... Oh, look here. I'm correct!
children.speaker: Yaming
children.cn: 嗯，我认为苔藓是最古老的植物之一。它大约在4.7亿年前出现在地球上……哦，看这里。我是对的！
children.notes: moss = 苔藓；appeared = 出现；correct = 正确的
children.statement: moss 是一种动物。
children.explanation: moss 是苔藓，属于植物。

children.text: Interesting! Let's go on!
children.speaker: Emma
children.cn: 有趣！我们继续吧！
children.statement: go on 意思是“停止”。
children.explanation: go on 的意思是“继续”。

children.text: Which kind of tree is the tallest in the world?
children.speaker: Yaming
children.cn: 哪种树是世界上最高的？
children.statement: tallest 是 tall 的最高级。
children.explanation: tall 的最高级是 tallest。

children.text: The redwood tree, right?
children.speaker: Emma
children.cn: 红杉树，对吧？
children.statement: redwood tree 是一种树。
children.explanation: redwood tree 是红杉树。

children.text: Yes! I hope I can see some one day.
children.speaker: Yaming
children.cn: 是的！我希望有一天能亲眼见到一些。
children.notes: hope = 希望；one day = 有一天
children.statement: hope 后面通常接不定式或宾语从句。
children.explanation: I hope I can see... 是 hope 接宾语从句的用法。

tree.text: Ella & Chen Jie's folding fan conversation

children.text: Introduction: Folding Fan
Oh, what's that, Chen Jie? It's so beautiful!
children.cn: 噢，那是什么，陈洁？它太漂亮了！
children.statement: 艾拉觉得陈洁手里的东西非常漂亮。
children.explanation: 艾拉赞叹道：'It's so beautiful!' (它太漂亮了！)。陈述正确。

children.text: Thanks. It's a folding fan. I bought it last year.
children.cn: 谢谢。这是一把折扇。我去年买的。
children.statement: 这把折扇是陈洁最近刚刚买的。
children.explanation: 陈洁说：'I bought it last year.' (我去年买的)。陈述错误。
children.speaker: Chen Jie
Ella

children.text: Bamboo in Chinese Culture
Oh! It has a painting of bamboo on it.
children.cn: 哦！上面画着竹子。
children.statement: 折扇上画的是牡丹花。
children.explanation: 艾拉说：'It has a painting of bamboo on it.' (上面画着竹子)。陈述错误。

children.text: Yeah. Bamboo is one of the most popular subjects in Chinese paintings. It's a symbol of goodness in Chinese culture.
children.cn: 是的。竹子是中国画中最受欢迎的题材之一。在中国文化中，它是美德的象征。
children.statement: 竹子在中国画中是很罕见的题材。
children.explanation: 陈洁提到：'Bamboo is one of the most popular subjects...' (竹子是最受欢迎的题材之一)。陈述错误。
children.speaker: Chen Jie
Ella

children.text: The Bamboo Sea in Sichuan
I didn't know that before. So, where did you get that fan?
children.cn: 我以前不知道这回事。那么，你从哪里买到这把扇子的？
children.statement: 艾拉以前就知道竹子在中国文化中的特殊含义。
children.explanation: 艾拉说：'I didn't know that before.' (我以前不知道)。陈述错误。

children.text: At the Bamboo Sea in Sichuan, the largest and oldest bamboo forest in China. I learnt lots of interesting things when I was there.
children.cn: 在四川的蜀南竹海，中国最大、最古老的竹林。我在那里的时候学到了很多有趣的事情。
children.statement: 蜀南竹海是中国最大、最年轻的竹林。
children.explanation: 陈洁说那是 'largest and oldest' (最大和最古老) 的竹林，而不是最年轻的。陈述错误。
children.speaker: Chen Jie
Ella

children.text: Interesting Bamboo Facts
Really? Like what?
children.cn: 真的吗？比如什么？
children.statement: 艾拉对陈洁学到的趣事不感兴趣。
children.explanation: 艾拉追问道 'Like what?' (比如什么？)，表现出极大的兴趣。陈述错误。

children.text: Well, bamboo is one of the fastest-growing plants. Some bamboo can grow almost one metre in one day. Also, it's one of the most useful plants in the world. People can use it to build houses and make tools and instruments.
children.cn: 嗯，竹子是生长最快的植物之一。有些竹子一天几乎能长一米。此外，它是世界上最实用的植物之一。人们可以用它来盖房子，制造工具和乐器。
children.statement: 竹子可以在一天内长高近一米。
children.explanation: 陈洁明确说：'grow almost one metre in one day' (一天内几乎能长一米)。陈述正确。
children.speaker: Chen Jie
Ella

children.text: Pandas & Food
Oh, that's interesting. I remember pandas love to eat bamboo too, right?
children.cn: 噢，那很有趣。我记得大熊猫也喜欢吃竹子，对吧？
children.statement: 艾拉提到熊猫喜欢吃竹叶和竹茎。
children.explanation: 艾拉提问大熊猫是否也爱吃竹子。陈述正确。

children.text: Exactly! Actually we eat bamboo shoots too. They're really delicious.
children.cn: 没错！其实我们也吃竹笋。它们真的很美味。
children.statement: 人类并不食用竹笋。
children.explanation: 陈洁说：'Actually we eat bamboo shoots too.' (实际上我们也吃竹笋)，它们很美味。陈述错误。
children.speaker: Chen Jie
Ella

children.text: Introduction: Supermarket Scenario
Imagine you go to the supermarket in your area, but there are almost no fruits, vegetables, honey, milk, eggs, or chocolate.
children.cn: 想象一下，你去了你所在地区的超市，但几乎没有水果、蔬菜、蜂蜜、牛奶、鸡蛋或巧克力。
children.statement: 这里假设超市里货物非常充足。
children.explanation: 文章让我们想象 'almost no fruits...' (几乎没有水果等)，说明是缺乏货物的场景。陈述错误。

children.text: How would you feel? Disappointed? Angry? Sad?
children.cn: 你会怎么想？失望？生气？伤心？
children.statement: 在这种情况下，人们通常会感到非常高兴。
children.explanation: 当超市里没有食物时，人们会感到失望、生气或悲伤。陈述错误。

children.text: At this moment you realize people, plants, and animals are all connected.
children.cn: 在这一刻，你意识到人、植物和动物都是相互联系的。
children.statement: 作者认为人与自然界是完全孤立、互不影响的。
children.explanation: 作者指出：'people, plants, and animals are all connected' (人类、植物和动物都是相互联系的)。陈述错误。

children.text: We need each other to live, and bees are one of the best examples of this connection.
children.cn: 我们需要彼此才能生存，而蜜蜂是这种联系最好的例子之一。
children.statement: 蜜蜂展现了人类、植物和动物之间紧密联系的最好例子之一。
children.explanation: 蜜蜂的授粉活动对植物生长及人类食物供应至关重要，是这一联系的极佳例证。陈述正确。

children.text: Pollination & Plant Growth
Bees are very hard-working animals, and their greatest work is pollination.
children.cn: 蜜蜂是非常勤劳的动物，它们最伟大的工作是传粉。
children.statement: 蜜蜂最伟大的工作是制作蜂蜡，而不是传粉。
children.explanation: 文中明确写道 'their greatest work is pollination' (它们最伟大的工作是传粉)。陈述错误。

children.text: On one trip, they often visit 50 to 100 flowers. As they fly from one flower to another, they take pollen with them.
children.cn: 在一次出行中，它们经常访问50到100朵花。当它们从一朵花飞到另一朵花时，它们随身带走花粉。
children.statement: 蜜蜂每次飞行往往只飞向一两朵花。
children.explanation: 文中指出它们每次出行 'often visit 50 to 100 flowers' (经常访问50到100朵花)。陈述错误。

children.text: This action helps plants grow. In fact, without their work, about 80 per cent of the plants in the world cannot grow.
children.keywords: helps grow, without work, 80 per cent
children.cn: 这种行为有助于植物生长。事实上，如果没有它们的工作，世界上大约百分之八十的植物都无法生长。
children.statement: 没有蜜蜂授粉，世界上大约80%的植物仍可照常生长。
children.explanation: 如果没有蜜蜂的授粉工作，世界上约有80%的植物将‘无法生长’ (cannot grow)。陈述错误。

children.text: For this reason, many scientists believe that bees are the most important animals on this planet.
children.cn: 出于这个原因，许多科学家相信蜜蜂是这个星球上最重要的动物。
children.statement: 因为蜜蜂对生态极为重要，科学家认为它们是地球上最重要的动物。
children.explanation: 由于授粉对植物生长的重要性，科学家认为蜜蜂是地球上最重要的动物。陈述正确。

children.text: Homes & Communication
Bees are also one of the most interesting animals in the world. For example, they work hard together to make their homes. In order to store honey, they create honeycombs.
children.cn: 蜜蜂也是世界上最有趣的动物之一。例如，它们共同努力建造它们的家园。为了储存蜂蜜，它们创造了蜂巢。
children.statement: 蜜蜂是单独行动，不会合作建造蜂巢的。
children.explanation: 文中提到 'they work hard together to make their homes' (它们共同努力建造家园)。陈述错误。

children.text: Another interesting thing about bees is how they communicate. When a bee finds new food, it uses a dance to tell the others where and how far away the food is.
children.cn: 蜜蜂的另一个有趣之处在于它们是如何交流的。当一只蜜蜂找到新的食物时，它会跳舞告诉其他蜜蜂食物在哪里以及有多远。
children.statement: 蜜蜂通过跳舞向同伴传递食物的位置和距离信息。
children.explanation: 蜜蜂通过舞蹈 (dance) 告知同伴食物的方向和距离。陈述正确。

children.text: Eco-Threat & Protecting Bees
Bees play an important part in the ecosystem of our planet. However, the number of bees is dropping, and scientists are worried.
children.cn: 蜜蜂在地球生态系统中起着重要作用。然而，蜜蜂的数量正在下降，科学家们对此感到担忧。
children.statement: 目前世界上蜜蜂的数量正逐年稳步上升。
children.explanation: 文中指出 'the number of bees is dropping' (数量正在下降)，这让科学家很担忧。陈述错误。

children.text: Bees need our help, and there is a lot we can do. Think about what you can do to protect one of the most amazing animals on earth.
children.cn: 蜜蜂需要我们的帮助，我们有很多可以做的事情。想一想你可以做些什么来保护地球上最神奇的动物之一。
children.statement: 作者呼吁我们采取行动保护蜜蜂。
children.explanation: 作者希望我们思考能做什么来保护蜜蜂这类神奇的动物。陈述正确。
